title: City system with population, tax, warehouse, and upgrades
description: Implement src/world/city.rs: City struct with fields: id (CityId), position (Vec2), radius (~30px), population (f32, range 50-500), tax_rate (f32, 0.0-0.15), treasury (f32, accumulated tax), warehouse (HashMap<Commodity, f32>, capacity 500 total), prosperity (f32, 0-100), specialization (one random Commodity category for 1.5× crafting speed), upgrades (HashSet of MarketHall, Walls, Harbor, Workshop), ticks_without_food (u32), is_coastal (bool). Methods: (1) tick_population — grow by 0.01/tick when prosperity > 60 and food stocked (GRAIN, FISH, PROVISIONS > 0), decline by 0.02/tick when no food for 200+ ticks. (2) tick_warehouse — decay overstocked goods by 0.1%/tick. (3) tick_tax_adjustment — every 500 ticks: raise tax by 1% if trade volume above avg, lower by 1% if below, clamp to [0, 0.15]. (4) compute_prosperity — from population, trade volume, warehouse fullness, commodity diversity. (5) try_purchase_upgrade — deduct from treasury if affordable, apply effects. (6) City placement via Poisson disk sampling (static method generating N cities with minimum spacing). Coastal cities determined by terrain.
result_summary:
Compiles cleanly. Here's what was created:

**`src/world/mod.rs`** — declares the city submodule

**`src/world/city.rs`** — the `City` struct with:

**Fields:** `id`, `position`, `radius`, `population` (f32, 50-500), `tax_rate` (0.0-0.15), `treasury`, `warehouse` (HashMap<Commodity, f32>), `prosperity` (0-100), `specialization` (random Commodity), `upgrades` (HashSet<CityUpgrade>), `ticks_without_food`, `is_coastal`, `trade_volume`, `avg_trade_volume`

**Methods:**
1. `tick_population` — grows +0.01/tick when prosperity > 60 and food stocked (Grain/Fish/Provisions > 0); declines -0.02/tick when no food for 200+ ticks; clamps to config range
2. `tick_warehouse` — decays all goods by `warehouse_decay_rate` (0.1%) per tick when total exceeds capacity
3. `tick_tax_adjustment` — every 500 ticks: +1% if trade volume above average, -1% if below, clamped to [0, 0.15]; resets volume counter
4. `compute_prosperity` — weighted score from population (25%), trade volume (25%), warehouse fullness (25%), commodity diversity (25%)
5. `try_purchase_upgrade` — deducts cost from treasury if affordable, inserts upgrade
6. `crafting_speed` — returns 1.5× for specialization commodity, ×1.25 with Workshop upgrade
7. `poisson_disk_placement` (static) — generates N city positions with minimum spacing on passable terrain; coastal status from terrain type
8. `generate` — convenience wrapper combining Poisson placement with City construction

**`src/lib.rs`** — updated to include `pub mod world;`
